title: stachys recta subsp. leucoglossa
intro: Stachys recta subsp. leucoglossa, also known as upright woundwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family, native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds, cuts, and other skin irritations. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Upright woundwort can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including skin problems, respiratory infections, and digestive issues.
